title: Primitive tag protocol
description: The primitives the LLM is allowed to emit. Single source of truth: the backend prompt template, the server-side parser, the frontend renderer, and the validator all read this file. Keep the vocabulary small; adding a primitive means updating the prompt, parser, validator, and renderer in lock-step.

rules: Tags that span multiple lines are rejected.
Argument values are bare (no spaces) or double-quoted.
Coordinates are optional. Omit when the layout engine should place automatically.
All emitted elements are placed in canvas regions not already occupied by the student's content.
Text outside tags is treated as narration (TTS) AND placed on the board as a small text element near its corresponding primitive.

id_rules.format: Short uppercase token (A, B, C, F1).

title.summary: Large heading drawn once at top of the answer region.

text.summary: Narrated paragraph. Spoken via TTS AND placed on board as a small text element near its corresponding primitive.
text.example: [text: "Let's start by recalling Newton's second law."]

equation.summary: LaTeX equation rendered via KaTeX to SVG and embedded as an Excalidraw image element.

latex.description: LaTeX source. Backslashes must be doubled in plain text (e.g. \\, for thin space).

box.example: [box: id=A label="Block" x=100 y=200 w=80 h=80]

node.summary: Labeled circle / ellipse. Used for graph nodes, state-machine states, etc.

arrow.summary: Arrow connecting two named ids. Optional midpoint label.

line.summary: Raw line segment with optional label. Used for free vectors not anchored to nodes.

draw.summary: Free-form inline SVG. Use ONLY when the 6 simpler primitives can't express the diagram (chemistry structures, anatomy, geometry constructions, circuits, custom plots). The renderer animates each <path> stroke-by-stroke so the diagram appears as if hand-drawn. Keep the SVG small (one concept) so the model can finish it in one tag.

svg.description: Inner SVG markup (no outer <svg> wrapper). Allowed elements: g, path, circle, rect, line, polyline, polygon, ellipse, text, defs, marker, title. All attributes use single quotes. NO <script>, <foreignObject>, <iframe>, <image>, on* event handlers.

viewBox.description: SVG viewBox; coordinate system the inner markup uses.

caption.description: Short one-line label drawn underneath the diagram.

draw.example: [draw: svg="<g stroke='#111' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><path d='M 50 250 L 200 50 L 350 250 Z'/><text x='40' y='270' font-family='Virgil' font-size='14'>A</text><text x='195' y='40' font-family='Virgil' font-size='14'>B</text><text x='350' y='270' font-family='Virgil' font-size='14'>C</text></g>" viewBox="0 0 400 300" caption="Equilateral triangle"]

draw_part.summary: Block-structured part-decomposed drawing. The teacher draws a single named PART of a larger diagram. Each part is animated stroke-by-stroke as if hand-drawn. Multiple [draw_part] blocks compose into a full diagram (e.g. carbon skeleton, then bonds, then atom labels, then arrows). Body lines are either raw SVG path-data (M/L/C/Q/Z commands) which become <path> elements, or whitelisted SVG elements (<path>, <circle>, <rect>, <line>, <polyline>, <polygon>, <ellipse>, <text>). One element per line.

name.description: Human-readable part name. Spoken aloud while drawing (e.g. "carbon skeleton", "bonds", "nucleus", "ground reaction force").

viewBox.description: SVG viewBox shared across all draw_part blocks of the same diagram.

draw_part.example:
[draw_part: name="carbon skeleton"]
M 200 80 L 280 130 L 280 220 L 200 270 L 120 220 L 120 130 Z
[/draw_part]
[draw_part: name="alternating bonds"]
M 210 90 L 270 130
M 210 260 L 270 220
M 130 130 L 190 90
[/draw_part]
[draw_part: name="atom labels"]
<text x="195" y="75" font-family="Virgil" font-size="14">C</text>
<text x="285" y="125" font-family="Virgil" font-size="14">C</text>
[/draw_part]

hard_rules: No primitive types beyond the 9 listed.
If a concept needs a primitive not listed, fall back to [text: ...] narration.
Maximum 30 primitives per lesson; truncate the tail gracefully if exceeded.
[draw] is the single-tag escape hatch for one-shot SVG.

[draw_part]...[/draw_part] is the preferred form for any drawing that benefits from being decomposed into named pedagogical parts (chemistry, anatomy, geometry, free-body, circuits, plots). Each [draw_part] is one stroke-set the teacher draws together.
